title: Redundant drop(cache) in CachingStore::get misleads about lock discipline
description: CachingStore::get() has an explicit drop(cache) inside a block that ends on the next line, making the explicit drop a no-op. Rust drops the guard at end of the block regardless. The explicit drop suggests the author was uncertain about Rust's scoping rules, which may cause a contributor to copy this pattern incorrectly (e.g., adding drop() calls where they think they need explicit unlocking). Remove the explicit drop() and let the block scope do its job.
